t_ch: 走一步看一步吧。
t_en: Let's feel it out.

t_ch: 嗯…眼下也没有更好的方法……
t_en: I guess we have to give it a go... I don't see any other options.

t_ch: 不过，我觉得，如果是尾巴，凭它张扬的个性…还真有可能在网上留下些踪迹。
t_en: I can't help thinking that if Tail is out there, there'll be traces of him on the Internet. He had a big personality...

t_ch: 你想找回尾巴吗？
t_en: Do you want Tail back?

t_ch: 嗯…{NICKNAME}会不会觉得我在自找苦吃呢？明明过去也不待见那家伙。
t_en: Yes... Do you think I'm just getting myself into more trouble? It's not like we were good friends to begin with...

t_ch: 可是它消失以后，我的尾巴…我是指自己真正的尾巴也不见了。镜子里的自己怎么看都变得不习惯了，连干活都没什么力气……
t_en: ...but when Tail disappeared, my tail — my real tail... it disappeared too. Now my reflection looks weird to me, and I hate work even more than before...

t_ch: ……说了许多借口，其实我只是很想念它。
t_en: ...M—Maybe that's all just a bunch of excuses... The truth is... I miss him.

t_ch: 我们会找到它的。
t_en: We'll find him.

t_ch: 谢谢你…如果想要找到它，那现在可能是个好机会了。
t_en: Thank you... if we're ever going to find Tail, now's the best chance we have.

t_ch: 它可能已经不在了。
t_en: He might not be around anymore.

t_ch: 嗯…可能是跑了，也可能是四分五裂，彻底不再是「尾巴」了…但无论如何，我想知道它的下落。
t_en: Mm... I guess so. Maybe he ran away, or broke into tiny pieces... Either way, I'd like to know what happened to him.

t_ch: 不过，眼下咱们的任务可不是找它。当务之急是尽快镇伏那些外逃的岁阳。
t_en: O—Of course, finding Tail isn't our top priority right now — we need to catch and subdue all the fleeing escaped heliobi as soon as possible.

t_ch: 尾巴…唉，当务之急是尽快镇压那些还在作乱的岁阳。
t_en: Tail... The important thing right now is stopping the heliobi from causing chaos.

t_ch: 小桂子这个人很跳脱，但这只是因为她思维活跃…办起正经事来，她还是相当可靠的。
t_en: Little Gui's methods can be a little unorthodox, but that's just because she has a vivid imagination... When it comes to getting things done, she's actually pretty reliable.

t_ch: 怎么突然冒出这么一句？
t_en: Why did you suddenly feel the need to say that?

t_ch: 哈哈，有点怕你信不过她。总而言之不会有问题的，小桂子在她擅长的领域可是无敌的。
t_en: Haha, I'm worried you don't trust her plan. Just relax — Little Gui is unbeatable in her area of expertise.

t_ch: 你们关系真好。
t_en: You two seem to have a lot of history.

t_ch: 你们不是总斗嘴吗？
t_en: You two sure like getting each other into trouble...

t_ch: 那都是我们友谊的增稠剂！
t_en: That's just the sticky glop that holds friends together!

t_ch: 是粘合剂好吧！
t_en: ...Glue.

t_ch: 唉，那天小桂子拉我来绥园，是希望我保护她的，结果我就…被吓晕过去了……
t_en: Like the time Guinaifen dragged me to Fyxestroll Garden so I could protect her, but I was so scared I ended up fainting...

t_ch: 因为我不在，之后这家伙又惹出了那么多乱七八糟的事…我没有扛起阻止她乱来的责任。
t_en: While I was passed out, she went and caused all kinds of trouble... I neglected my duty of keeping her from making a mess of things.

t_ch: 我一定要一雪前耻！这次一定要保护好…嗯，还有看管好小桂子！
t_en: But I'll redeem myself this time! I'll make sure to protect her— uh, and also keep an eye on her...

t_ch: 话说回来，你为什么还要回来淌这趟浑水啊？岁阳可不好对付啊……
t_en: That being said, why did you decide to come back into this mess? Dealing with heliobi isn't an easy task...

t_ch: 似乎也没人征求过我的同意…
t_en: I'm not sure I was given the choice...

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，对哦！好像确实没人问过你肯不肯加入这个捉鬼小队，就默认你是小队的一员了。
t_en: Hahahaha, oh yeah! No one asked you if you wanted to join the ghost hunting squad — we just took you in by default.

t_ch: 要不你再考虑一下？现在后悔还来得及！
t_en: Sure you don't wanna reconsider? It's not too late to change your mind!

t_ch: 来都来了…
t_en: Well, since I'm already here...

t_ch: 说得好！来都来了！
t_en: Good point! May as well see it through!

t_ch: 之前和浮烟有点过节…
t_en: I have a score to settle with Cirrus...

t_ch: 哦对，你好像也被岁阳附身过来着？那滋味应该不太好受吧？
t_en: Oh, that's right, you were possessed by a heliobus once before — guess it wasn't a pleasant experience, was it?

t_ch: 附身时间太短，不过瘾。
t_en: It was too short — I want to try it again.

t_ch: 得了吧你，别嘴硬了。
t_en: C'mon, don't act tough.

t_ch: 是不太好受。
t_en: Not great.

t_ch: 哎呀，真是辛苦你了。没事，咱这次一起去给你报仇！让岁阳见识见识得罪错人的下场！
t_en: I'll bet, poor you... This time we're gonna avenge you! I'll show those heliobi what happens when they mess with my friend!

t_ch: 我不干净了！！！
t_en: I'M TAINTED!

t_ch: 吓我一跳…没事，咱这次一起去给你报仇！让岁阳见识见识得罪仙舟人民好朋友的下场！
t_en: Ah! Can you not do that...? Don't worry, this time we're gonna avenge you! I'll show those heliobi what happens when they mess with my friend!

t_ch: 这不过是一份工作罢了。
t_en: You come for me, you best not miss.

t_ch: 这台词像是戏里酷劲十足的保镖会说的话。
t_en: What are you, a mob boss?

t_ch: 我就不能像你说的这么轻松了…毕竟我见到这些家伙腿肚子都会打颤，上一次还晕了过去，哈哈。你…不会笑话我吧。
t_en: You're brave, I'll give you that... Just seeing a heliobus makes me weak in the knees. You... won't laugh at me if I faint, right?

t_ch: 总之，一想到这些妖怪到处乱跑，我还是没法置身事外。尤其是它们之前还把我和小桂子吓得半死，这事可不能就这么算了。
t_en: Ugh, I can't stand the thought of those things roaming free. After what Little Gui and I went through, I'm not about to let them have their ghostly way.

t_ch: 我一定要一雪前耻！这次一定要保护好小桂子！
t_en: I have to redeem my honor this time! I have to protect Little Gui!

t_ch: 把如今罗浮的将军带到我面前，咱们或许可以谈谈。
t_en: Bring me the current general of the Luofu, and we can talk.

t_ch: 几位不妨先行一步，待批阅完手头的公文，我会立刻前往绥园。
t_en: As soon as I finish my paperwork for the day, I will make my way to Fyxestroll Garden. Please, go ahead of me.

t_ch: 你们…完事了吗？
t_en: Are you done running around yet?

t_ch: 暂时没有。
t_en: Not quite.

t_ch: 只是和你打个招呼。
t_en: I'm just stopping by to say hi.

t_ch: 想我们啦？
t_en: Miss us?

t_ch: 呸，别太得意忘形了！你忘了自己可是曾被我凭依操纵，玩弄的不亦乐乎的傀儡吗？
t_en: Hah, spare me! Have you forgotten that I once controlled you like a puppet?

t_ch: 我已按约为你们提供了线索，怎么还是空手而归？
t_en: I provided you with information as promised — why am I still empty-handed?

t_ch: 哼，若不是为了再战一场的事就别来烦我！
t_en: Tsk, don't bother me again until you're ready to fight!

t_ch: 不过嘛，我打赌，你们之后一定会来求我的，咱们走着瞧。
t_en: You'll be begging for a rematch later... just you wait.

t_ch: 岁阳本是无根自在之火，可惜却因一念好奇，为人类所囿。可惜。
t_en: The heliobi are like a flame that burns eternal, with no need for sustenance. But alas, their curiosity got the best of them, and they became prisoners of humans. It is a pity.

t_ch: 将军同情它？
t_en: Do you sympathize with them?

t_ch: 与其说同情，不如说是关照。
t_en: ...I lament that we share the same weaknesses.

t_ch: 我们与岁阳的处境并无不同，从拥有灵智的那一刻起，受欲念和本性驱使，注定要被束缚在自己的命途上，步向自己的结局。
t_en: Our situation is no different from that of the heliobi. From the moment we gain sentience, we are driven by desire and instinct, bound to our destiny, moving unceasingly towards our own demise.

t_ch: 你就不怕咱们被打败…
t_en: Aren't you afraid we'll be defeated by them...

t_ch: 持节将兵，最重要的是对胜败的直觉。而各位令我有必胜的信心。
t_en: The most important thing in a soldier is one's intuition for victory or defeat. And you have given me confidence in victory.

t_ch: 浮烟为何执着于要与将军决斗…
t_en: Why is Cirrus so obsessed with fighting the general of the Luofu?

t_ch: 按古老的神话所载，仙舟曾面临大敌入侵，举族覆灭的绝境。为此，有位凡人英雄为了挽救危亡，不惜与岁阳之祖「燧皇」立下夺舍约定。
t_en: The ancient legends recount that the Xianzhou once faced an invasion by a great enemy that threatened the annihilation of every race aboard the Xianzhou ships. In order to save the Alliance, a hero made a pact with the Flint Emperor — the predecessor of all heliobi.

t_ch: 燧皇借出力量助战，而凡人与其追随者们若能凯旋，将会永远失去自己的身体。
t_en: The Flint Emperor would aid the Xianzhou in battle, and if they were victorious, the hero and their followers would lose their physical bodies for eternity.

t_ch: 那场大战结束后，英雄们无一生还，而燧皇也受了重创。这场交易便不了了之。人们也逐渐遗忘了这个故事。但岁阳却还记得。
t_en: When the fighting had ceased, none of the heroes survived, and the Flint Emperor was gravely wounded. Thus, the pact was unable to be fulfilled. As the centuries came and went, the story faded from memory. But the heliobi remember.

t_ch: 它们索偿此债，永世不忘。腾骁将军与燎原的大战，多半也是为此而起。
t_en: They hold claim to this debt for all time. The great battle between General Teng Xiao and Ignamar was fought largely for this reason.

t_ch: 您不回神策府，没事吗？
t_en: You're not going back to the Seat of Divine Foresight?

t_ch: 「协助十王司镇伏岁阳。」这可是个特别正当的理由。
t_en: Aiding the Ten-Lords Commission in subduing the heliobi is a legitimate reason to take a leave of absence.

t_ch: 就当我给自己放个假吧，好好欣赏一下绥园，虽说这里的风真是透体寒凉啊。
t_en: I shall treat it as a vacation and enjoy the scenery of Fyxestroll Garden — though I must admit, the wind here does chill the bones.

t_ch: 唉…这龙女大人怎么动不动就跑…我能不能申请个「恶劣环境作业补贴」啊。
t_en: *sigh* Why does the Dragon Lady always have to run off... I swear they should be paying us extra for this...

t_ch: 就你屁话多。嫌苦你就申请调岗到随军医士吧。
t_en: Hah! I should get worker's comp for listening to you complaining all day. If you don't like the work, you can always transfer to the military.

t_ch: 哼，竟派出四个卒子羞辱我…
t_en: Hmph, trying to humiliate me with lowly pawns...

t_ch: 我自然也有我的棋子——
t_en: No matter then, I have my pawns to play...

t_ch: 棋子们，听我号令！
t_en: Heed my command!

t_ch: 我赐予你们力量！
t_en: Receive my power!

t_ch: 让你们见识见识岁阳的奇术！
t_en: Now you will experience the might of the heliobi!

t_ch: 怎么会突然消失……
t_en: How could she suddenly disappear...

t_ch: 难道是闹鬼了？
t_en: Maybe it was a ghost?

t_ch: 也可能是云吟术……
t_en: Maybe it was cloudhymn magic...

t_ch: 你别说，还真有可能……
t_en: You know what, that might actually have been the case...

t_ch: 能听到笑声，但完全找不到人在哪里……
t_en: I can hear laughter, but where's it coming from...?

t_ch: 连我也开始觉得有点瘆人了……
t_en: Ugh, now I'm starting to get the heebie jeebies...

t_ch: 对吧对吧，真的吓人。
t_en: You can say that again...

t_ch: 门后怎么是这么个地方？
t_en: I never thought this would be behind the door...

t_ch: 只是岁阳的障眼法罢了……
t_en: Remember, it's just a heliobus illusion...

t_ch: 喂，那边那个岁阳，快把白露大人放了！
t_en: Hey, you over there! Release Lady Bailu!

t_ch: 捣乱的家伙，咱们俩本来快快乐乐地在这儿玩耍呢！阿露她不想跟你们回家！
t_en: Don't be a bunch of fun-suckers! She doesn't wanna let anyone take her away!

t_ch: 阿灼，他们是来找我的。天色都暗了，我是不是该回去了？
t_en: Luxie, they came here looking for me... It's starting to get dark, maybe it's time for me to go back now?

t_ch: 阿露，咱们不是说好了吗，不让任何大人找到！也绝不跟他们回家。你…你怎么这么快就反悔了呀？
t_en: Lulu, didn't we say before that we wouldn't let any grown-ups find us? And never let them take us home? Why... why did you change your mind?

t_ch: 呃…你说得有道理！大人可以说话不算话，但咱们不能。
t_en: Hmm... you're right! Grown-ups break their promises, but we don't!

t_ch: 就是就是！你饿了的话，我可以给你变出吃不完的琼实鸟串；你渴了，我这儿有喝不完的星芋啵啵！
t_en: That's right! Remember, if you ever get hungry, I can conjure up anything you like — berrypheasant skewers, startaro bubble tea — you name it. We have it right here!

t_ch: 好耶！快变，快变！
t_en: Okay! Make some right now!

t_ch: 现在还不行喔。咱们先一起玩个游戏吧？几个坏家伙，听见了吗？你们也有份！
t_en: Not yet — why don't we play a game first? You bad guys over there, okay? You're gonna be in the game too!

t_ch: 阿露，咱们俩逃，只要不被这些坏大人抓到，我就把好吃的好喝的统统变给你！
t_en: Lulu, let's make a run for it — if we escape from them, I'll conjure up all of your favorite snacks and drinks!

t_ch: 好哇，那还等什么呢，开跑！
t_en: Wow! Then what are we waiting for? Let's go!

t_ch: 自由自在！一起玩到天昏地暗！
t_en: We're free from the grown-ups! We can play all day every day!

t_ch: 别跑！
t_en: Wait, stop!

t_ch: 帝弓在上…这家伙跑得也太快了……
t_en: Whew... By the Arbiter... They sure know... how to run...

t_ch: 这是…岁阳的…力量吧！
t_en: That's... the power of a... heliobus!

t_ch: …你先喘口气，别昏过去了……
t_en: ...Catch your breath... before you pass out...

t_ch: 咱们…咱们开个作战会议吧…我觉得这么穷追猛打不是个办法，要用脑袋……
t_en: Let's... let's try a new strategy... I don't think we can beat them like this. We need to use our heads...

t_ch: ……连裳裳这样的体力派也忍不住要用脑袋了！
t_en: ...Sushang's... gonna use her head...? Now we're really done for...

t_ch: 我能跑上一整天！
t_en: I barely broke a sweat.

t_ch: 那你…很棒棒哦！但是，可不可以把嘴角的白沫咽回去先。
t_en: Then... good for you! But you should probably catch your breath first... I could hear you panting from a mile away.

t_ch: 我也不行了！
t_en: I think I'm hyperventilating.

t_ch: 家人，你醒醒！把嘴角的白沫咽回去啊！
t_en: Ha, you're fine! Just take a breather...

t_ch: 就没人随身带个陷阱吗？
t_en: Anyone bring a hunting trap?

t_ch: 这又不是打猎，伤到白露小姐该怎么办？
t_en: Huh? We can't hunt the Dragon Lady like a wild beast...

t_ch: 等等，我有个法子！反过来试试如何？不去追逐白露小姐，而是等她自己跑过来！
t_en: Wait, I have an idea! How about we use a little reverse psychology? Instead of chasing Lady Bailu, we get her to come to us!

t_ch: 小桂子你说什么胡话呢，这小龙人像是会自己冲我们跑过来的样子吗？
t_en: Are you serious? Does that little dragon girl look like she's gonna come running over to us?

t_ch: 眼下的捉迷藏，让我想起小时候生活的那颗行星上，资源匮乏，想要吃点肉就得冒上巨大的危险，深入矿坑去狩猎一种叫浮鼬的动物。
t_en: Chasing Bailu around like this reminds me of something I saw back home when I was a child. On my home planet, resources were scarce, and if you wanted to eat meat you had to risk your life by going deep into the mines to hunt an animal called a sky mink.

t_ch: 浮鼬飞起来灵活的很，而且警觉，身手再好也很难抓住。
t_en: Sky minks fly so nimbly and are so alert that even the quickest of hands will have a hard time catching them.

t_ch: 怎么突然开始讲故事了？
t_en: Why are we having story time all of a sudden?

t_ch: 那你们后来吃没吃上肉？
t_en: Did you get the meat? How does the story end?

t_ch: 又到了小桂子的童年故事模式了。
t_en: Gather round children!

t_ch: 别打岔，听我讲完。浮鼬的生态很特别，它会与一种叫鸣蜥的毒物共生在一处。外出觅食时，就让鸣蜥来保护自己的幼崽。
t_en: Ugh, don't interrupt! Hear me out. The sky mink's ecology is unique — it lives in symbiosis with a venomous creature called a shrieking lizard. When a sky mink goes out to feed, it lets the lizard protect its young.

t_ch: 所以我们就去鸟窝里抓鸣蜥。那家伙被抓住后，会用尖锐的叫声示警浮鼬回巢保护幼崽。不过这样一来，它也就落入了咱们手里。
t_en: So we used to go to sky mink nests to catch shrieking lizards, instead. When a shrieking lizard gets caught, it calls to the sky mink with a loud shriek to warn it to return to the nest and protect its young... which of course, led the sky mink right to us.

t_ch: 有点残忍…
t_en: Sounds a little cruel...

t_ch: 生存竞争是很公平的。鸣蜥的鼓囊和眼球会喷出剧毒，防护工作干得不好，许多小伙伴都把命搭了进去。
t_en: Well, the competition for survival went two ways — shrieking lizards spew out a potent venom, and failure to take proper precautions cost many a hunter their life.

t_ch: 好聪明…
t_en: Sounds pretty smart...

t_ch: 也谈不上聪明。人为了活下去，什么难事都愿意做。鸣蜥的鼓囊和眼球会喷出剧毒，很多小伙伴都是为了吃上一口肉，把命给搭进去了。
t_en: I'd hesitate to call it that. Shrieking lizards spew out a potent venom — many people lost their lives trying to catch a tiny morsel of meat for dinner.

t_ch: 所以，听了半天，这故事和你说的法子有什么关系啊？
t_en: Nice story. But what's it got to do with chasing Lady Bailu?

t_ch: 我都讲得这么明白了！抓住其中一个，另一个就会自投罗网！
t_en: Isn't it obvious? If we can catch one Bailu, the other will come running to us!

t_ch: 我还记得之前判官大人手里有个名叫「藏月瓠」的宝贝，能吸锁岁阳…呃，就是被我打碎的那个…不知还在不在？
t_en: I remember one of the judges had a precious item called a Lunaumbra Gourd, which could absorb and imprison heliobi... Uh, come to think of it, I think that's the thing I smashed... I wonder if they fixed it?

t_ch: 葫芦我确实随身带着，但也得在岁阳靠得近时才能用上啊。
t_en: Actually, I have one with me, but it's only effective when a heliobus is close by.

t_ch: 要是你拿着藏月瓠在某个死角守株待兔，而剩下的人将白露小姐赶来这儿。
t_en: Nice! In that case, you can take the gourd and camp out in a corner — the rest of us will chase Lady Bailu back over here.

t_ch: 咱们抓住了白露小姐，那个岁阳就会现身落入我们的陷阱吗？岁阳是这么重情重义的东西吗？
t_en: If we catch Lady Bailu, will Luxie really follow her into our trap? Do heliobi really have a sense of loyalty to their victims?

t_ch: 谁知道呢？浮烟不是说阿灼是小孩子心性吗？就试试看吧。
t_en: Who knows? Cirrus said that Luxie is kinda childlike — it's worth a try.

t_ch: 放我出去！我不想被关在这里！
t_en: Lemme go! I don't wanna be locked in here!

t_ch: 白露大人…保持冷静，你被岁阳依附了，你的所思所想都不是你自己的念头。
t_en: Lady Bailu — please calm down. You're not yourself right now — you're being influenced by a heliobus.

t_ch: 胡说！胡说！我从小到大都没有什么玩伴，今天难得遇到一个，你们却要抓它……
t_en: You're lying! I never had playmates. Today I finally found one, and you guys are trying to take them away...

t_ch: 大人真是卑鄙啊，连小朋友难得的快乐时光也要夺走！
t_en: Grown-ups are so mean! As soon as they see children having fun, they wanna ruin it!

t_ch: 嘻嘻！我很卑鄙吧！
t_en: Hee-hee! I'm a big meany head!

t_ch: 噫，对、对不起！
t_en: You're right. Sorr... Sowwie!

t_ch: 快醒醒，别被它激发了愧疚的情绪。
t_en: Careful! Don't let them manipulate you.

t_ch: 老大，咱还是放了人质吧？
t_en: Yo boss, maybe we should let her off with a warning this time?

t_ch: 快醒醒，别被它激发了愧疚的情绪。
t_en: Careful! Don't let them manipulate you.

t_ch: 哇，这家伙果然自投罗网了。
t_en: Phew, sure enough, they walked right into our trap.

t_ch: 本想将你们一起带入快乐自由的儿童世界，不过既然你们执意要煞风景…就别怪阿灼用暴力来和你们对话啦！
t_en: Hmph, I was considering letting you join the club and have fun with us... but since you're so determined to spoil everything... I'm afraid we'll have to settle our differences through violence.

t_ch: 暴力，只有暴力才是你们这些大人唯一会说也唯一能听懂的语言，对吧！？就像你们每次教训孩子时那样！
t_en: Yes, violence, the only language you grown-ups know how to speak, right? The one you use when you're telling us off!

t_ch: 呜，对不起，阿露…我、我被大人教训了，我不能陪你玩了……
t_en: I'm sorry, Lulu... Th—The grown-ups won, I can't play with you anymore...

t_ch: 阿灼…不是拉勾说好了，绝不跟大人回去吗？大人可以说话不算话，小孩子这样也可以吗？
t_en: Luxie, we said we'd never go back with the grown-ups! They break their promises, but we can't!

t_ch: 可是，我也没办法呀。再长的嬉戏，也总有结束的那一刻。
t_en: There's nothing I can do about it... playtime can't go on forever...

t_ch: 不过，今天玩的很开心，下次再约吧！
t_en: ...but we still had lots of fun — let's play again next time!

t_ch: 没有下一次啦！藿藿，拜托了。
t_en: There won't be a next time! Huohuo, you know what to do.

t_ch: 阿灼，下次见面…大概要很久以后了……
t_en: Luxie, how long before the next time we can play together?

t_ch: 咱们的人生很长的啦，从这个尺度上来看，不会有你想象得那么久～我暂时得跟这些大人走啦！
t_en: We both have many years ahead of us, so in that sense it won't be long at all... but for now I have to leave with these grown-ups!

t_ch: 好啦…白露小姐，时候不早了，该是回家的时候了。
t_en: Alright, alright. Lady Bailu, it's getting late — you need to go home now.

t_ch: 随着岁阳的消失，白露半睁半闭的眼睛渐渐恢复了清醒。
t_en: As the heliobus disappears, Bailu's eyes regain their clarity. She has retaken control of herself.

t_ch: 虽然记忆有些模糊，但我之前好像做了很多奇怪的事情……
t_en: I... everything's so fuzzy... Did I do something bad?

t_ch: 不用愧疚，白露小姐，就当是做了一场自由玩耍的梦吧，这不是你的错。
t_en: No need to feel bad, Lady Bailu. You were in a dreamworld — none of it was your fault.

t_ch: 可是…可是，大家为了我到处奔波…我还记得，真对不住。
t_en: But... but... I made everyone chase after me for so long... I shouldn't have done that...

t_ch: 被人管束，被人禁锢，就会想着要逃离，这不是天性吗。是那些总想着纠正行为的大人，那些觉得你该做什么的人有问题才是。
t_en: It's only natural to want to escape when strict rules are being forced on you. I know there are a lot of grown-ups trying to control you — telling you what you can and can't do... they're the ones to blame.

t_ch: 我都准备好了道歉的说辞…大姐姐，你把我的思路打乱了。
t_en: I was ready to apologize to everyone... but now you've made me forget what I wanted to say.

t_ch: 干嘛要道歉啊？我和你一样，虽然已经快成年了，不过还总是忍不住怀念小时候光着脚到处乱跑的日子。
t_en: Apologize for what? You know, you and me have a lot in common. I'm almost a grown-up now, but I still miss my childhood days and running about barefoot.

t_ch: 被大哥训斥、被要求干这干那、被要求成熟一点，接下来是反抗和逃跑，为了证明自己离开家人也能过得很好…所以，我在仙舟安顿了下来。
t_en: My big brother used to boss me around... He'd give me chores, constantly telling me to grow up, that kind of thing... Then one day, I decided to rebel. I ran away to prove that I could get by without my family... and eventually settled on the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 看来龙女也好，我这样的普通人也罢，大部分人生活的轨迹都很相似嘛。
t_en: Whether it's a Dragon Lady like you or an ordinary person like me, I feel like many people's lives follow a similar trajectory.

t_ch: 总有一天，白露小姐你也会有力量离开眼下这段人生，前往下一段生活的…所以我建议你好好珍惜它眼前的乐趣，而不要反复为它的烦恼而道歉。
t_en: One day, you'll have the strength to leave your current situation and move on to the next phase... In the meantime, I suggest you try and cherish the good rather than apologize for the bad.

t_ch: 大姐姐，你还挺会给人灌心灵鸡汤的嘛，不如来丹鼎司的医馆挂牌当个心理医士吧？
t_en: Hehe, alright, alright, I don't want everyone to feel too sorry for me... Hey, did you ever think about joining the Alchemy Commission as a psychotherapist?

t_ch: 呜，她真的是个逃家的孩子吗？怎么说起话来夹枪带棒的，好早熟啊。
t_en: Geez, and there I was pouring my heart out... Now I feel like this kid's lived longer than I have...

t_ch: 我说咱们还是别、别在这儿呆着了。这地方也太阴森了…咱们换个地方聊天好不好？
t_en: Umm, c—can we not linger around here too long? This place is really spooky... Maybe we can chat somewhere else?

t_ch: 藏月瓠已经设置好了，只要把白露小姐赶到这儿，我们就能抓住她……
t_en: The Lunaumbra Gourd is in place. If we can chase Lady Bailu to this spot, we should be able to catch her...

t_ch: 那…那个…一定要注意安全呀！
t_en: Umm... b—be careful, okay?

t_ch: 我就是想多出来玩一玩！我有什么错？
t_en: I just wanna play a little longer! What's so bad about that?

t_ch: 为什么我要按大人的方式来生活呢？
t_en: Why do I have to live the way grown-ups do?

t_ch: 大人们总说：「现在不要贪玩，等长大后再玩。」
t_en: Grown-ups are always saying "study now and have fun later..."

t_ch: 可我看丹鼎司那些大人也没时间玩啊！
t_en: ...but the grown-ups I see at the Alchemy Commission never have time for fun!

t_ch: 不要再追啦！
t_en: Stop chasing us!

t_ch: 让我开开心心玩一会儿不好吗！
t_en: Can't you just let me have some fun!?

t_ch: 大人们总是说大人的生活更辛苦，可这很奇怪诶？
t_en: Why do grown-ups always say that life is harder for them. So weird...

t_ch: 大人不也是从小孩子变来的吗？
t_en: Weren't grown-ups kids once too?

t_ch: 为什么要和过去的自己去比较谁更辛苦呢？
t_en: Would you spoil the fun for younger you just because older you isn't happy?

t_ch: 就是就是！
t_en: Exactly!

t_ch: 为什么总要把这种我听不懂也理解不了的事情说给我听啊？
t_en: Why do they always talk about things I don't understand!

t_ch: 我只是想出去玩罢了！
t_en: I just wanna have fun!

t_ch: 关上前面那扇门…她就只能往那个方向逃了……
t_en: That door over there is the only way out... We'd better close it...

t_ch: 一个临时的想法来了，你应该<color=#dbc291ff>关上这扇门</color>试试。
t_en: An idea pops up: You should try <color=#dbc291ff>closing this door</color>.

t_ch: 一个灵感突如其来，你应该<color=#dbc291ff>打开这扇门</color>试试。
t_en: A flash of inspiration hits: You should try <color=#dbc291ff>opening this door</color>.

t_ch: 如果世界上存在「正确」的命途，一定会对你投下赞许的目光吧。
t_en: If there exists the Path of "Correction" in this world, then they would surely cast approving glances upon you.

t_ch: 这里房间四通八达，<color=#dbc291ff>要不要关上其中几扇门呢？</color>
t_en: The rooms here are interconnected in all directions. <color=#dbc291ff>Should you close some of the doors?</color>

t_ch: 万事俱备，去吧，<color=#dbc291ff>将白露赶进藏月瓠的阵法之中</color>吧！
t_en: All is set. Now go on, <color=#dbc291ff>drive Bailu into the matrix of the Lunaumbra Gourd</color>!

t_ch: 万事俱备，去吧，<color=#dbc291ff>将白露赶进藏月瓠的阵法之中</color>吧！
t_en: All is set. Now go on, <color=#dbc291ff>drive Bailu into the matrix of the Lunaumbra Gourd</color>!

t_ch: 白露预判了你堵死路的想法，只要她跑得快，就可以为所欲为。
t_en: Bailu anticipated your attempt to block her path, but as long as she runs fast enough, she can do whatever she wants.

t_ch: 你好啊，{NICKNAME}，咱们开始今天的行动吧？
t_en: Hello. Let's get started with today's operations.

t_ch: 十王司下辖岁阳逃逸事件特别处理行动小组，简称捉鬼小队，又要振作精神开始干活了！耶！
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission Special Operations Team for the Heliobus Containment Breach Incident — A.K.A the ghost-hunting squad — is ready for action! Yeahhh⁓!
Yeah...

t_ch: 耶！
t_en: Yeah!

t_ch: 看来只有咱俩还算精神…
t_en: Looks like we're the only ones in good spirits today...

t_ch: 滴，打卡。
t_en: Clocking in...

t_ch: 为什么你的眼神和地衡司那些执事一样疲惫？
t_en: Yo, why the exhaustion? You look like the officers over at the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 居然不是弹性工作时间？
t_en: Can I switch to a four-day week?

t_ch: 招工启事上从来没有说过这一点吧？不对，咱们哪儿有招工启事啊。
t_en: I don't think that was included in the job posting. Actually, I don't think there ever was a job posting.

t_ch: 困死了，小桂子你是吃了什么灵丹妙药，这么精力充沛？
t_en: I'm sleepy... Little Gui, what kind of magical elixir did you take to make you so energetic?

t_ch: 每次看到粉丝数蹭蹭往上跳，我就兴奋得睡不着，比喝下什么浓茶咖啡都管用。
t_en: Every time I see our follower count increase, I get so excited I can't even sleep — it works better than any kind of tea or coffee.

t_ch: 你是干这个的，我可不行。只要我一闭上眼，就梦见自己和白露小姐不断周旋周旋周旋…比亲自上阵还累人。
t_en: Well aren't you lucky. As soon as I closed my eyes, I dreamt of endless running and circling and chasing after Lady Bailu... it was even more exhausting than doing it in real life.

t_ch: 裳裳，你都睡了一整个白天了！
t_en: Sushang, you slept for an entire day!

t_ch: 抓鬼是个晚上出洞的活计，睡过一整个白天岂不是很合理。
t_en: Catching ghosts is a night job — it only makes sense to sleep during the day.

t_ch: 不如这样，你们就当我是睡魔缠身，把我镇压在床上，让我再睡上三五十个时辰，就当是向十王司交差了，如何？
t_en: I have an idea — how about we all just pretend that I've been possessed by a sleep ghost, let me sleep for another ten hours, and then call it a day's work?

t_ch: 这摆烂发言让我想起某人…
t_en: You sound like someone I know...

t_ch: 嗯，很像是被太卜司那位著名的牌手上了身。
t_en: A certain Celestial Jade-playing Divination Commission employee, perhaps...?

t_ch: 太卜司的牌手？太卜司不都是算卦的么。
t_en: Divination Commission? Celestial Jade? Shouldn't they be doing... divinations?

t_ch: 素裳，你认识青雀吗？
t_en: Sounds like you've spent time with Qingque...

t_ch: 啊，就是那位长乐天著名的牌手！我每次执行任务经过那儿，总能见到她雷打不动地和牌友们围城大战。
t_en: That's the Celestial Jade player over at Exalting Sanctum! Whenever I pass through on official business, I always see her with loads of other players.

t_ch: 连判官大人也知道她！她还真是闲名远播啊。
t_en: Even a judge knows her! Her reputation sure does precede her...

t_ch: 到底是谁啊！
t_en: Who is this, exactly?

t_ch: 就是这个人啊。你们看这个帖子。
t_en: It's this girl. Here, look at this post.

t_ch: …我工作还是很认真的好吧！至少不会理直气壮写什么「照例迟到」。
t_en: ...I take my job seriously! I'd never post something like "late as usual" on a public forum.

t_ch: 素裳小姐的关注点好奇怪啊。
t_en: Miss Sushang, I think you missed the interesting part...

t_ch: 这条消息里的描述好离奇啊，似乎很符合岁阳寄生作祟的情况。如果连太卜司这样的机要部门也被岁阳占据，那可就麻烦了。
t_en: The description in this comment is really suspicious, it definitely sounds like heliobi activity to me... and if heliobi have managed to infiltrate the Divination Commission, that spells big trouble for the Luofu.

t_ch: 确实…感觉应该先联系一下青雀小姐，提醒她不要上班。
t_en: You're right... Maybe we should contact Miss Qingque and warn her not to go to work today.

t_ch: 她还用提醒？
t_en: Since when does she need reminding to skip work?

t_ch: 为了避免有人因为好奇心靠近危险区域，咱们还是得履行告知义务。
t_en: That's a good idea. We should send out a message, or people's curiosity will get the better of them.

t_ch: 我来吧。
t_en: I'll do it.

t_ch: 你们将阿灼束缚在了阵法中…我还听见了其他岁阳在低鸣、震颤…渴望离开此处。
t_en: So, you imprisoned Luxie in the matrix... and I heard the cries of other heliobi longing to break free.

t_ch: 十王司的进展很顺利嘛。现在你们来见我，想必是又有所求咯？
t_en: It would seem the Ten-Lords Commission has everything under control... What is it you want from me?

t_ch: 老样子。
t_en: Same old same old.

t_ch: 只是来找你撒气。
t_en: Just came to vent some anger.

t_ch: 你该知道，你动不了我分毫的对吧？
t_en: Outside the confines of our agreement, you would be unwise to attack.

t_ch: 想你了。
t_en: We miss you.

t_ch: 我承认你成功恶心到我了。
t_en: You have managed to disgust me still further...

t_ch: 按照将军与你所立的约定，一场胜利换一个问题。
t_en: One victory equals one question... don't forget the agreement you made with the general.

t_ch: 胜不胜利的，在战斗开始前可别那么笃定啊，小妹妹。没有尾巴帮你，收服岁阳的工作是不是有些力不从心了？
t_en: Don't be so sure of victory before the battle begins, young one. Without Tail to protect you, isn't subduing a heliobus a bit overwhelming for you?

t_ch: 只要你敞开心接纳我…我可以代替尾巴，我可以帮你。
t_en: You know, if you can accept me with an open heart... I can take his place and help you.

t_ch: …真、真的吗？
t_en: ...R—Really?

t_ch: 当然了。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 那就好。
t_en: That's what I wanted to hear.

t_ch: 好什么？
t_en: Oh?

t_ch: 你在竭尽全力蛊惑我，看来你对胜负根本也没什么把握。
t_en: The fact that you're trying so hard to persuade me means that you're not sure of victory either.

t_ch: 要是你还有几分胜算，早就不顾一切地对我们几个露出獠牙了。我说的没错吧，浮烟？
t_en: If you were, you'd have devoured us without hesitation. Am I right, Cirrus?

t_ch: 那么，如你所愿。上枰厮杀吧，景元的棋子们！
t_en: Then have it your way — I'll destroy Jing Yuan's pawns here and now!

t_ch: 这一次，还是我们胜了。
t_en: We did it...

t_ch: 有什么话就问吧。
t_en: Ask your question.

t_ch: 太卜司似乎被某个岁阳盘踞了，不知道你有什么头绪？
t_en: We think the Divination Commission has been infiltrated by a heliobus, but we don't know which one.

t_ch: 你们向浮烟讲述了太卜司的现状。
t_en: You tell Cirrus what's happening at the Divination Commission

t_ch: 会去往仙舟人窥测未来的地方…除了「犀焰」之外，应该不会有其他人了。
t_en: Entering the Xianzhou's window to the future... It must be none other than Keralum.

t_ch: 我知道它最爱纠缠那些先知和预言家们，喜欢与他们诘辩命运，看他们在随机和命定间痛苦徘徊。
t_en: They always relished hounding your prophets and seers with questions of destiny — watching them flounder between the random and the pre-ordained.

t_ch: 命运…是注定的。
t_en: Destiny... is pre-ordained.

t_ch: 命运…不是注定的。
t_en: Destiny... is not pre-ordained.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，你们人类脑壳里装的那坨蛋白质，计算效率实在太低，你们对命运的猜测只是类比和错觉罢了。
t_en: Hahaha, that pile of protein in your human skull is computationally inefficient — your conjectures on destiny are little more than analogies and illusions...

t_ch: 但咱们无形目不一样，当我们聚在一起共振然后观测，命运就像举火照明般清晰可见。
t_en: ...but we, The Shapeless, are different. When we come together to harmonize and observe, destiny is clear and vivid as firelight cast into darkness.

t_ch: 与人争斗，能预测对手的进退行动，这样的能力最重要不过——所以，聚成「燎原」的岁阳中，犀焰司掌着鉴往知来，预测休咎的职能。
t_en: In warfare, nothing is more important than being able to predict your opponent's movements. Among the fragments of Ignamar, Keralum was in charge of knowing the past and predicting the future.

t_ch: 不过，据说犀焰曾预感到了失败的前兆，却不知为何，燎原还是无视了它的警告。
t_en: It is said that during that fateful battle, Keralum had a premonition of failure, but Ignamar ignored their warnings.

t_ch: 看起来这次的目标十分明确。咱们出发吧。
t_en: It looks like we have a clear target this time. Let's get going.

t_ch: 虽然我没有犀焰那般本领，但是，小狐狸…我预言，你迟早有一天会祈求我的帮助。
t_en: I may not have the same predictive power as Keralum, little fox girl... but one thing I do know is that sooner or later, you will beg for my help.

t_ch: 走着瞧吧！
t_en: Time will tell!

t_ch: 唷，你们来了啊！怎么连十王司的判官也来了？
t_en: Oh, you're here! Is that a Ten-Lords Commission judge?

t_ch: 里面情况怎么样？
t_en: What's the situation inside?

t_ch: 我跟你讲，现在太卜司里面状况不太对啊，所有人都念念有词的，是准备职场下克上了吗？该不会是因为我无意中起了坏榜样的作用吧？
t_en: Something's amiss in the Divination Commission — everyone is mumbling to themselves. Is this some workforce overhaul? Did I set a bad example?

t_ch: 青雀小姐也太会联想了。不，这件事纯属意外。但要解释起来……
t_en: I—I'm afraid not, Miss Qingque. Something unexpected happened... I'm just not sure how to explain it...

t_ch: （如实相告）
t_en: (Tell the truth.)

t_ch: 太卜司可能被某种不明生物盘踞了，要是贸然闯进去，你也会成为受害者！
t_en: The Divination Commission may have been infiltrated by some unknown creature... If you rush in, you could become a target too!

t_ch: 素裳小姐，你忘了咱们行动的保密原则了吗！
t_en: Miss Sushang, we're supposed to be operating in secret!

t_ch: 轻描淡写反而会让青雀小姐意识不到眼下的状况有多危险。
t_en: I want Qingque to have an idea of the gravity of the situation.

t_ch: 算了，那东西应该拿青雀小姐这样无欲无求的性格也没法子吧。
t_en: Hmm... I suppose Miss Qingque should be safe enough — the one we're chasing probably has a more... complicated target...

t_ch: 虽然像是夸我，但听着…哎嘿，就当是在夸我吧。
t_en: That sounds like a compliment, but at the same time... Eh, I'll just take it as a compliment.

t_ch: （敷衍一下）
t_en: (Avoid saying too much.)

t_ch: 反正…反正眼下太卜司里出了些危险的状况，你不要进去就对了！
t_en: Well... anyway, there's a dangerous situation at the Divination Commission at the moment, so just don't go in there!

t_ch: 工作条件这么恶劣啊，叫人怎么专心工作嘛。
t_en: Good idea, with working conditions like these, I wouldn't be able to concentrate anyway...

t_ch: 我听工造司的阿伟说了，他们司部里也有个什么玩意儿泄漏了。好几个学徒都开始胡言乱语起来，超吓人…当然，也可能是出不了师毕不了业导致的。
t_en: I heard from Awei that the Artisanship Commission is also experiencing strange phenomena — several of the apprentices have started babbling nonsensically, it's kinda scary... Of course, it could just be a side-effect of being denied graduation.

t_ch: 那就拜托青雀小姐在这儿守着。
t_en: In that case, Miss Qingque, could you stand guard here for us while we investigate?

t_ch: 里头这么危险，我在这儿守着，不合适吧？
t_en: It doesn't seem like a good idea to just stand here under such dangerous circumstances, does it?

t_ch: 青雀小姐如果想离开，也不必为难…不对，难不成你要和我们一块进去吗？你也看到太卜司的情况了，咱们这一队人是专为解决此事而来的。
t_en: I mean, you could leave, but... Wait, are you thinking of coming with us? Didn't you see how bad the situation was...?

t_ch: 是是，都看到了。可我要是现在转身走了，不就变成旷工了吗？上班摸鱼归摸鱼，旷工可就不为太卜所容了。
t_en: Yeah, yeah, I've seen it alright... but if I leave now, won't that count as unauthorized absence? I can get away with slacking off, but the Master Diviner won't tolerate complete abandonment of my post.

t_ch: 再说，你们也需要一个熟悉太卜司的人来给你们带个路，搭把手什么的，对不对？咱们走吧。
t_en: Besides, you guys need someone familiar with the Divination Commission to show you the way, right? Then let's go.

t_ch: 他们这是在看什么呢？
t_en: What are they looking at?

t_ch: 大家…还好吗？
t_en: Is everyone... okay?

t_ch: 道路永远只有一条…
t_en: There is only one way...

t_ch: 卜算毫无意义…
t_en: Divination is meaningless...

t_ch: 穷观阵，怎么变成了这样……？
t_en: How did the Matrix of Prescience become like this?

t_ch: 你们看，符太卜站在穷观阵的中心，她在做什么？
t_en: Look, Diviner Fu is standing at the center of the Matrix of Prescience... what is she doing?

t_ch: 看她念念有词的模样，应该是跟过去一样，演算着航路或未来……
t_en: Judging by her chanting, I think she's performing navigation calculations, or divining...

t_ch: 但穷观阵却纹丝不动，不对劲，很不对劲。
t_en: But something's not right — the Matrix of Prescience isn't moving at all.

t_ch: 她应该已经被岁阳凭附了…
t_en: She's probably already been possessed by a heliobus...

t_ch: 岁阳…就是你们所说的危险吗？
t_en: A heliobus... is that the danger you were talking about?

t_ch: 我在书里读到过，「能自在幻化的星火之精，喜好操弄凡物的梦想和欲望」。你是说太卜被那种东西控制了…？
t_en: I read about them in a book. "Starfire Essence that can transform itself — enjoys manipulating the dreams and desires of mortals." You mean the Master Diviner is being controlled by something like that...?

t_ch: 该怎么才能让她醒过来？
t_en: How can we snap her out of it?

t_ch: 摇一摇「同心火铃」试试。我们在离太卜大人这么近的地方摇动铃铛，想必能进入她与岁阳共有的幻境中。
t_en: We could try ringing the Harmonic Chime... If we're close enough, we should be able to enter the heliobus's illusion.

t_ch: 不过，在走之前，我得帮这几位解决眼下太卜司面对的麻烦。要不然，别人还以为我是临危脱逃才被开除的呢。
t_en: But before I leave, I have to help these guests take care of some trouble here at the Commission. If I don't, people will think I was fired because of cowardice in the face of danger.

t_ch: 肃静！太卜司内，不得聒噪！
t_en: Silence! Idle chatter is not permitted in the Divination Commission!

t_ch: 你醒了啊，符玄大人！
t_en: Master Diviner, you're awake!

t_ch: …还是我们眼下该称呼你「犀焰」了？
t_en: ...Or perhaps we should call you Keralum?

t_ch: 你们怎么称呼我并不重要，无论符玄还是犀焰，都是这太卜司唯一的主人。
t_en: It doesn't matter what you call me — I am the master of the Divination Commission.

t_ch: 眼下占卜的工作正进行到重要关头，容不得他人扰乱。就算是十王司的判官，星穹列车的客人，也不得在这儿放肆！
t_en: The current divination has reached a delicate stage, and no one is permitted to disturb it — not even a judge of the Ten-Lords Commission or a guest from the Astral Express!

t_ch: 青雀！又是你，是你把这些外人带进司部的吗？你把我平日里所立的规矩都当作耳旁风了吗？
t_en: Qingque! I have grown weary of your insubordination! Are you the one who brought these outsiders into the Commission? Have you turned a deaf ear to all the rules I've laid down?

t_ch: 青雀，你被开除了！
t_en: Qingque, you are hereby expelled from the Commission!

t_ch: 我…？这…这也…
t_en: I...? Th—That's...

t_ch: 可恶…一下子就让咱们减员一人！
t_en: Darn... Now we're down one person!

t_ch: …这也太棒了吧！
t_en: ...a great idea!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我也觉得自己镇日游手好闲地不成体统，一边打牌一边拿着太卜司的薪俸…实在有些，那句话怎么说来着……
t_en: Eh-he, my days here really have been unproductive — loafing around outside and playing Celestial Jade — all while taking a Divination Commission salary. It's... how to put it...

t_ch: 「不劳而获」？
t_en: Compensation without labor?

t_ch: 倒也不是，毕竟交给我的事儿都有按时完成……
t_en: Not exactly, I do complete all my assigned work on time...

t_ch: 「禽兽食禄」？
t_en: Living high on public expense?

t_ch: 虽然不知道哪里不对，但这个词用在这儿肯定不太妥当……
t_en: That's not quite the right way to describe it...

t_ch: 「爽到不行」？
t_en: Too much of a good thing?

t_ch: 我也觉得，这样的日子，小桂子我做梦都想过。
t_en: Sure sounds like it. I could only dream of having a cozy job like that.

t_ch: 是「浪费生命」。谢谢太卜大人推了我一把，既然您也觉得我不该留在这儿大材小用，我是该想法子做些改变了。
t_en: I would call it: Wasting my life away. Thank you, Master Diviner, for giving me the push I needed. Since you feel that my skills could be put to better use elsewhere, it's time for me to think about making some changes.

t_ch: 不过，在走之前，我得帮这几位解决眼下太卜司面对的麻烦。要不然，别人还以为我是临危脱逃才被开除的呢。
t_en: But before I leave, I have to help these guests take care of some trouble here at the Commission. If I don't, people will think I was fired because of cowardice in the face of danger.

t_ch: 接下来，接下来该如何是好？
t_en: What next? What do we do now?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，光顾着说些帅气的话了，到底怎样才能让太卜从走火入魔中脱离出来啊？
t_en: Hehe, giving a dramatic speech to my boss was cool and everything, but it's not gonna help her break free from the heliobus clouding her mind.

t_ch: 我也不知道。那个岁阳看起来不打算用迷境困住咱们，似乎也不想攻击我们……
t_en: I'm not sure what to do... The heliobus isn't trying to trap us inside the illusion, and it doesn't look interested in attacking us either...

t_ch: 走火入魔？本座很清醒，不如说从未如此清醒过。
t_en: "Clouding my mind"? I've never been of clearer mind than I am right now.

t_ch: 从现在开始，本座将全心全灵致力于推演仙舟联盟注定的未来，并将这些未来昭告天下，让人们都活得明明白白。
t_en: From now on, I will dedicate myself to divining the predetermined futures of the Xianzhou Alliance, and will make these futures known to the world so that all can live in clarity.

t_ch: 你说这话就不太清醒…
t_en: I think you've gone off the deep end...

t_ch: …怎么可能推算出全部未来？
t_en: ...How is it possible to divine every future?

t_ch: 哪有什么注定的未来？
t_en: Since when is the future predetermined?

t_ch: 本座知道你要说什么。「人定胜天」、「自由意志」…但这些都不过是人类大脑产生的简单幻觉。从一开始，道路就只有一条。
t_en: I know what you want to say. Spare me the lecture on "human wisdom prevailing over nature" and those ridiculous notions of "free will"... These are but crude illusions created by the brain. From the beginning, there was only one path.

t_ch: 你，{NICKNAME}，你不过是一颗被星核猎手摆布的棋子罢了。
t_en: You, {NICKNAME}, you are nothing more than a pawn at the mercy of the Stellaron Hunters.

t_ch: 登上列车，与谁为敌，去往何处……这些你以为自己可以听凭心意自由做出的选择，不过是命运的道路上终将汇合的支流。
t_en: What day you boarded the Express, who your enemies are, where you go... These choices that you think you make freely of your own accord are mere tributaries... that will eventually converge on the path of destiny.

t_ch: 帮助仙舟击败幻胧的这次旅行，想必你深有体会吧？所有的结果早被那位隐于幕后的奴隶书写成了剧本。
t_en: Your journey to the Xianzhou, the defeat of Phantylia — all these were written into a script long ago by that slave to destiny, hidden in the shadows.

t_ch: 就像眼下，你所有的选择不过是——「挥舞球棒，向我冲来」、「大声却徒劳地驳斥我」、「一语不发地陷入沉默」这么几种。
t_en: Even in this very moment, your options are few: Ready your bat and charge at me, argue with me loudly and futilely, or fall into silence.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，别被她言中了！总会有其他选择的！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, don't let her words get to you! There are always other options!

t_ch: （挥舞球棒，向符玄冲去）
t_en: (Ready your bat and charge at her)

t_ch: 运势涨落在离坎之间，寸功不竟，你不可能战胜我。
t_en: The rise and fall of the tides are dictated by the fire trigram and the water trigram — you are powerless against such a force. You have no hope of defeating me.

t_ch: （大声却徒劳地驳斥符玄）
t_en: (Argue with her loudly and futilely)

t_ch: 符玄的眼神中透露着失望。
t_en: Fu Xuan seems disappointed.

t_ch: 你想清楚再来找我。
t_en: Come back to me when you've thought it through.

t_ch: （一语不发地陷入沉默）
t_en: (Fall into silence)

t_ch: 符玄的眼神中透露着失望。
t_en: Fu Xuan seems disappointed.

t_ch: 你想清楚再来找我。
t_en: Come back to me when you've thought it through.

t_ch: 这些微不足道的选择，无法改变必然的命运。
t_en: Such trivial choices cannot change the inevitable.

t_ch: 如果你还不服气，本座可以拨冗为你演示究竟何为命中注定——用你人类的脑袋所能理解的方式。
t_en: If you're still not convinced, I can demonstrate to you the true nature of destiny — in a way that your human brain can comprehend.

t_ch: 来吧，我也奉陪。
t_en: Okay, sounds good to me.

t_ch: 那就随我移步吧。
t_en: Then follow me.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent.)

t_ch: 如果你理解了关于命运的真相，现在离开还不迟，别再打扰本座的演算了！
t_en: If you have gained clarity on the true nature of destiny, you may leave... and stop interfering with my divinations!

t_ch: 来吧，你可要看好了。
t_en: Alright, make sure you're paying attention.

t_ch: 太卜大人！你…你还好吗？
t_en: Master Diviner! A—Are you alright?

t_ch: 我…我没事。只是有些头疼。
t_en: I... I'm fine. Just a small headache, nothing more.

t_ch: 被岁阳寄生后，会有些不适的，不过服些丹药应该就能无恙了。
t_en: It's normal to experience some discomfort after being possessed by a heliobus, but nothing a few medicinal pellets can't take care of.

t_ch: 这团火就是犀焰吗？虽然没什么力量，但要是被缠上还真会令人陷入无止尽的绝望中呢。
t_en: Is that... Keralum? Hmph, they may not be the most powerful fragment, but becoming entangled in their illusion can lead to endless despair.

t_ch: 快些…快些将我…收入黑暗中……
t_en: Hurry... just hurry up and... seal me in the darkness...

t_ch: 我看到了，你们将我…收入了囚牢里……
t_en: I've foreseen what happens next... you're going to imprison me...

t_ch: 青雀，多谢你力挽狂澜。
t_en: Qingque, thanks to you, the Divination Commission is safe again.

t_ch: 不客气啊太卜。对了，您刚刚在岁阳寄体时把我开除了，这个决定应该是动真格的吧。接下来要劳烦您老人家下达一纸书面通知，单方面解除合同。
t_en: No need to thank me, Master Diviner. By the way, while you were possessed, you fired me from the Commission, so... that counts, right? I'll have to ask you to give me written notice that my contract has been unilaterally terminated.

t_ch: 这样一来，以我在太卜司混的这些年资，能拿到一笔不小的赔偿呢…呼呼。
t_en: Then, with all the years I've spent in the Divination Commission, I'll be able to get a great severance package...

t_ch: 我都想好了，我要用这笔钱去开个牌馆，然后雇几个小工，终日食利，不劳而获展开新生活，哈哈。
t_en: ...and I already planned what to do with all the money — I'm gonna open a gaming parlor, hire a couple of workers to run it, and spend the rest of my days living off the profits. I'll have started a new life without having lifted a finger, hehe.

t_ch: 这和刚才的青雀小姐根本就不是一个人吧？
t_en: Are there two Qingques?

t_ch: 唉，真是遗憾，本座之前说了不少胡话，早已一句都不记得了。
t_en: I see... What a pity, I don't recall anything I may or may not have said while possessed by the heliobus.

t_ch: 说起来，青雀，你为什么还在这儿，眼下你不该在书库值勤吗？
t_en: In any case, Qingque, why are you still here? Shouldn't you be on duty back at the repository?

t_ch: 啊？！太卜一诺千金，怎么能反悔呢？
t_en: Huh!? M—Master Diviner, you can't back out now!

t_ch: 当然可以，毕竟人生「总会有选择的」，这可是你教我的啊。
t_en: Of course I can. After all, there are always choices in life. You taught me that.

t_ch: 唉，真是拗不过。您先歇会儿，我摸…我是说我干活去啦。
t_en: I can't convince real you, let alone possessed you... Master Diviner, you should get some rest. I'm gonna go take a br— uh, I mean... get back to work...

t_ch: 符玄强撑精神四处检视，不用多时，太卜司勉强恢复了秩序。
t_en: Fu Xuan brings herself to inspect the commission, and order is soon restored.

t_ch: 做出了很多荒唐的事，真是颜面尽失。
t_en: My actions today brought great shame on the Divination Commission...

t_ch: 所幸其他人没有受伤。
t_en: At least no one else got hurt.

t_ch: 嗯，只有本座受伤的命运达成了。
t_en: Yes, all is well, minus my headache...

t_ch: 幸好除了青雀，没人瞧见。
t_en: Don't worry, Qingque's the only witness in the Commission.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，本座得好好想想封口的法子了…说笑罢了。
t_en: Hmm, I shall have to think of a way to keep her from talking... Hehe, just kidding.

t_ch: 岁阳擅长顺着人的弱点趁虚而入…无论是谁都有弱点，这不是符玄大人的错。
t_en: Heliobi are good at finding people's weaknesses and exploiting them... Everyone has some kind of weakness — you're not to blame.

t_ch: 是啊，符玄大人只是受害者，千万不必自责了。
t_en: That's right — you were just a victim of the heliobus's trickery, Diviner Fu — no need to blame yourself.

t_ch: 衷心感谢各位的帮助。
t_en: I must express my sincere gratitude to all of you for your help.

t_ch: 也许有些不礼貌，但太卜司横遭磨难，本座还要回去主持大局，恕不远送。
t_en: Apologies, but the Divination Commission is in disarray at the moment. I must return and set things in order — I'm afraid I cannot see you off in person.

t_ch: 啊！稍等一下，还有一件事。
t_en: Wait! There's something we need to ask you.

t_ch: 什么事？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 你们向符玄解释了这次行动的细节。
t_en: You tell Fu Xuan the details of your operation.

t_ch: 「太卜被岁阳附体」这件事传出去不好听，我明白了。
t_en: Mm, I agree that "Master Diviner Gets Possessed" wouldn't make for a good headline...

t_ch: 本座倒是不在意你们怎么编派，只要隐去姓名和身份就行了。
t_en: I don't care how you spin the story, so long as you leave my name and identity out of it.

t_ch: 本座还有很多工作要处理，今天的恩情，就请容许本座留到他日再答谢吧。
t_en: I have many things to attend to — please allow me to save my thanks for another day.

t_ch: 那就不打扰了，接下来咱们也该回绥园把犀焰封禁起来了。
t_en: We won't delay you any longer. We need to go to Fyxestroll Garden and seal Keralum away.

t_ch: 驱除幽厉，拿捉精灵。在十王司冥差的协助下，你们封印了「藏月瓠」内岁阳。
t_en: Expel evil and capture stray spirits... With the support of the Ten-Lords Commission's spiritfarers, you seal the heliobus within the Lunaumbra Gourd.

t_ch: 看来你们的行动还挺顺利的。
t_en: It seems that your operations have met with some success.

t_ch: 我也在浏览「罗浮杂俎」上的种种奇闻轶事，有趣的很。
t_en: I've been browsing through some of the stories on Ghostly Grove myself — they make for good reading.

t_ch: 这么说你知道太卜的事？
t_en: Did you hear about the Master Diviner?

t_ch: 所谓善易者不卜。常年为仙舟卜算吉凶，难免会产生诸多心魔，被岁阳趁虚而入。好在有你们的帮助，符卿已安然度过此劫。
t_en: As the saying goes, serious divination concerns serious matters. It is inevitable that someone performing divination day in and day out for the Xianzhou would acquire inner demons, and heliobus can take advantage of those. Fortunately, with your help, Fu Xuan prevailed over her intruder.

t_ch: 你怎么看待命运？
t_en: What do you think about destiny?

t_ch: 你喜欢听人说书，看幻戏吗？
t_en: Do you enjoy listening to storytellers and watching immersia?

t_ch: 说书也好，幻戏也罢，所有故事的结局都是注定的，你该不会因此而放弃享受这些故事吧？
t_en: In both cases, tales have a predetermined ending — but you shouldn't let that stop you from enjoying them, should you?

t_ch: 人生和命运也是同样的道理。无论结局是否注定，都值得一过。
t_en: The same is true of life. Whether the end is predestined or not, it's worth living.

t_ch: 先走了。
t_en: I'm heading off.

t_ch: 不打扰了，寒鸦小姐正在镇妖塔等着你们呢。
t_en: Very well, then. Miss Hanya is waiting for you by the suppression towers.

t_ch: 各位代表十王司弭平了太卜司中的灾异，功勋彪炳，令小女子也与有荣焉。我会上秉十王，表彰诸位。
t_en: On behalf of the Ten-Lords Commission, I commend you for your great service in resolving the Divination Commission crisis. I will honor all of you before the Ten Lords.

t_ch: 谢谢寒鸦大人的赞美。
t_en: Thanks, Madam Hanya...

t_ch: 这一次全都是靠了青雀小姐的帮助，咱们才能顺利收伏犀焰。
t_en: ...but we really couldn't have defeated Keralum without Miss Qingque's help.

t_ch: 没想到如符玄大人这般神机妙算的人杰，也会受岁阳蛊惑。
t_en: I didn't realize that one as skilled as Diviner Fu would also be susceptible to the power of the heliobi.

t_ch: 话说回来…我也能理解她的苦恼。毕竟我们都是在各自看不见的道路上分毫不差地前行着。
t_en: That said... I can understand her plight. After all, we are all moving forward on our own unseen paths... in no uncertain terms.

t_ch: 判官奉行十王诰谕，执行命令，却从没有人向我们解释其含义……
t_en: Judges are instructed by the Ten Lords to carry out their orders, but no one ever explains their meaning to us...

t_ch: 就像太卜大人无论看到何等残酷的、可怕的选择，只要有利于罗浮众生，她都要选择。
t_en: Just like the master diviner — no matter what cruel and horrible paths she foresaw, as long as it benefited the Luofu, she had to make a choice.

t_ch: 也许最终这些决策都是正确的，高瞻远瞩的，但我们毕竟也是血肉凡胎，还是会为做出选择的过程不由得感到痛苦。
t_en: Those choices may be correct and far-sighted, but we are, after all, flesh and blood, and we cannot help but agonize over the process of making them.

t_ch: 寒鸦大人……
t_en: Madam Hanya...

t_ch: 抱歉，小女子走神了，想必是案牍工作有损心识。今日之事已经完毕，还请各位好好休息吧！
t_en: I apologize, I was getting carried away. I think all the paperwork must have worn down my mental acuity. Today's business is finished. Please get some good rest!

t_ch: 对！今天就先解散吧，判官大人也是，可别把自己给累坏了。
t_en: Right! Let's call it a day. And you too, Judge Hanya — don't work yourself to death.

t_ch: 终于可以又躺回床上了。各位告辞啦。
t_en: It's finally time to get back to bed. Goodbye, everyone.

t_ch: 我也累坏了，晚些在群里聊吧。
t_en: I'm totally beat, too. Chat more in the group later.

t_ch: 记得回去分享一下账号情况。
t_en: Remember to share the account updates.

t_ch: 寂寞了？无事可做了？皮痒了？
t_en: Lonely? Nothing to do? Shall I wake you up?

t_ch: 有什么话就快说吧！
t_en: If you have something to say then say it!

t_ch: 你怎么看待命运？
t_en: What do you think about destiny?

t_ch: 如果你敞开心扉接纳我，我可以带你去见证你的命运喔？
t_en: If you open your heart to me, I can take you to witness your destiny.

t_ch: 好啊。
t_en: Okay~

t_ch: 算了吧，我不喜欢两次品尝同一道菜，也不喜欢在同一个肉体里呆两次。
t_en: ...Forget it. I don't like tasting the same dish twice — the same goes for possessions.

t_ch: 滚。
t_en: Take a hike.

t_ch: 是你自己来搭话的嘛。
t_en: You're the one who brought it up.

t_ch: 既然你想聊命运？那么不妨告诉你，我从来不去想那种东西。
t_en: If it's destiny you wish to discuss, I might as well tell you that I never think on it, or anything of the sort.

t_ch: 世间万物都有结束的那一天。可能兆亿年后，也可能明天下午，可能是因为纳努克的卒子，也可能是虚无的一声哈欠，或者其他什么理由……
t_en: Everything in this world will come to an end. It could be a trillion years from now, or tomorrow afternoon. It could be as a result of Nanook's pawns, or a sigh from the Nihility, or something else entirely...

t_ch: 这个宇宙总会走向终结，什么长生种短生种都要一起完蛋。这个是不可动摇的事实。
t_en: Eventually, this universe will be over, and all the species — long and short-life together — all of them will end up in the same place. It is inevitable.

t_ch: 可是知道了这个事实又能怎么样呢？它和你有什么关系？
t_en: But does the knowledge of this fact change anything? What does it have to do with you?

t_ch: 对于火焰来说，我不会因为要在宇宙热寂时熄灭就停止眼下的燃烧；就像你不会因为明天仙舟要沉没而放弃今晚的晚饭。
t_en: If you knew the Luofu were to perish tomorrow, would you skip dinner tonight? The flame of the heliobi will be extinguished in the hot silence of the universe... but that won't stop me from burning today...

t_ch: 命运…鱼儿为何要操心河流流向哪儿？
t_en: Destiny... why should a fish worry about where the river flows?

t_ch: 浮烟神游物外，不再回应你的提问。
t_en: Cirrus seems lost in thought, not answering any more questions.

t_ch: 没事，就过来看看。
t_en: I just came by to look around.

t_ch: 我也许该向你收参观的票钱了。
t_en: You should be paying me for the privilege.

t_ch: 时来天地皆同力，运去英雄不自由……
t_en: When times are good, heaven and earth work in harmony. When times are bad, even heroes struggle for freedom...

t_ch: 真抱歉，刚刚发了牢骚…
t_en: My apologies for rambling on just now...

t_ch: 今日之事已经完毕，请各位好好休息吧！
t_en: Today's work is finished! Please make sure to get some rest!

t_ch: 通知完毕，是不是该去太卜司看看情况了？
t_en: Notification sent. I guess now we should go to the Divination Commission and see what's going on?

t_ch: 老规矩，先试试向浮烟探探情况。
t_en: Mm, but let's talk to Cirrus again first.

t_ch: 不必为上一次胜利感到窃喜…
t_en: Don't let your previous victory go to your heads...

t_ch: 因为只要你们稍有闪失，我就会乘虚而入！
t_en: ...the slightest misstep will be your undoing!

t_ch: 没有景元在身后看着，是否底气不足了？
t_en: Let's see how you perform without the general here to watch your back...

t_ch: 我会不断试探，不断挑战。
t_en: I will never rest...

t_ch: 你们会疲劳、会气衰，而我不会。
t_en: Your mortal bodies will fatigue and breathe their last breath... and I will endure.

t_ch: 一切都是命中注定。
t_en: Everything is predetermined.

t_ch: 卜算已经没有意义了。
t_en: Divination has become meaningless.

t_ch: 卜算已经不存在了。
t_en: Divination is done for.

t_ch: 世界上没有任何变数。
t_en: There are no variables in this world.

t_ch: 道路永远只有一条。
t_en: There is only one way.

t_ch: 这是怎么回事…
t_en: What's going on...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，离我近、近一点好吗？我、我有点害怕……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, s—stay close to me, okay? I'm... I'm a little scared...

t_ch: 发泄愤怒毫无意义。
t_en: Venting your anger is meaningless.

t_ch: 愤怒也不能改变既定的命运。
t_en: Anger cannot change that which is already ordained by destiny.

t_ch: 只有进攻，没有防御，这便是你的战术吗？
t_en: All offense, no defense. Is that your strategy?

t_ch: 你的攻击不会落到我身上，只能殃及这些无辜的傀儡。
t_en: Your attacks won't land on me — only on these innocent puppets.

t_ch: 我看透了！我看透了！
t_en: I see through your trickery! I see through every bit of it!

t_ch: 我会失败的！我不可能胜利！
t_en: I'm going to lose! Victory is beyond my reach!

t_ch: 为何，为何没有第二条路可走？
t_en: Why... why is there no alternative?

t_ch: 宇宙的一切早已被决定好了！
t_en: Everything in this universe is predetermined!

t_ch: 不，我…我不可能赢过他们…
t_en: No, I... I had no path to victory...

t_ch: 这里是…？
t_en: What is this...?

t_ch: 这是太卜司里咱们管它叫「寻径指津」的沙盘，是司部成员专用来推演理路，锻炼头脑用的游戏。太卜把咱们带到这儿来的用意是……？
t_en: This place is what the Divination Commission calls the "Pathfinder." It's a sandbox game that diviners use to simulate future paths and exercise our minds. But why did the Master Diviner bring us here?

t_ch: 你想和我们玩游戏？
t_en: You want to play a game with us?

t_ch: 这就是你要展示的命中注定？
t_en: Is this where you show us the true nature of destiny?

t_ch: 没错，「听我说」，{NICKNAME}。在这片小小迷宫中放入机巧，对其下令，它便会按令寻路，走向出口。这与身在命运之中的人类何其相似？
t_en: That's right. Now listen, {NICKNAME}. If you place an ingenium in this little maze and give it a command, it will find its way to the exit precisely as it was commanded to do so. Are humans not the same as they navigate the path of destiny set before them?

t_ch: 不过我猜你会回答「人并非机器」「世界不只一条道路」！
t_en: But you're probably thinking: "humans are not machines, there is more than one path in the universe!"

t_ch: 人不是机器。
t_en: Humans are not machines.

t_ch: 世界上又不止一条路！
t_en: There is more than one path in the universe!

t_ch: 我们不过是更复杂更精密的机器罢了。
t_en: And yet machines we are — complex, sophisticated, but machines.

t_ch: 我们和这迷宫中的机巧一样，看不见太多的选择…我们只能循着最短的路线，在唯一的道路上穿行。
t_en: We are just like the ingenium in this maze — we have no options to speak of... We can only navigate one path — the one that takes us to our destination.

t_ch: 开始吧，把机巧引领到沙盘的出口，让我看看你们是怎么「选择命运」的。
t_en: Let's begin. Lead the ingenium to the exit. Show me how many choices destiny gives you.

t_ch: 总感觉我们好像被拉进岁阳的节奏里了……
t_en: It feels like we're just dancing to the heliobus's tune...

t_ch: 我们解出来了！
t_en: We did it!

t_ch: 很好，你们的选择正如法眼所见。你们应该明白，这个谜题自始至终只有唯一的答案，这就像命运的道路只有一条！
t_en: Well done. Your choices are as the omniscia foresaw them. You should know that there was only one answer to this puzzle from the beginning, just as destiny has only one path!

t_ch: 这只是个游戏。
t_en: It's just a game.

t_ch: 这道题是由你设计的。
t_en: This puzzle was designed by you.

t_ch: 没错，对于一个一开始就被设计好的谜题来说，任何其他选择都是毫无意义的错误。
t_en: Yes, for a puzzle that was designed from the start, choosing any other path would be a pointless mistake.

t_ch: 但宇宙并不是谁的游戏，也没有人为我们设计谜题等待解答，宇宙不存在唯一的解…身在其中的我们可以做任何事。
t_en: But the universe isn't someone's game, and no one is designing puzzles for us to solve. The universe doesn't have a single answer... we can do anything we want while we're in it.

t_ch: 哪怕是同样一天的工作，我可以躲在书库看闲书，也可以找人打牌。这就是自由。
t_en: Even if it's the same work every day, I can hide in the library and read a book or find someone to play Celestial Jade with... and that's freedom.

t_ch: 这么摆烂的事情能这么理直气壮的说出来，还让人觉得好有道理…太卜司的卜者，真有文化！
t_en: I never thought slacking off could convince me of anything... the sign of a true scholar! A diviner of the Divination Commission after all!

t_ch: 裳裳醒醒啊！你快要被摸鱼的岁阳侵占脑瓜了！
t_en: Sushang, snap out of it! A slacker heliobus is getting into your head!

t_ch: 青雀啊青雀，对于你而言，人生可以有看似无穷多的选择。那是因为，你的每一种选择，与你的每一天同样都是毫无意义的虚度。但对我来说，选择将会带来截然不同的后果。
t_en: Qingque, Qingque... For you, life may have a seemingly infinite number of choices. That is because your choices are simply different ways of slacking off and wasting time — they are of no consequence to anything... but for me, choices come with very real consequences.

t_ch: 呃，还真是太卜式毫无情商的发言呢……
t_en: That's the most "divinery" thing you've said so far...

t_ch: 本座身为罗浮仙舟的太卜，身负重责。我必须日复一日、日复一日地捋清纷繁复杂的命运之线，才能保全这座巨舰。
t_en: As the Master Diviner of the Luofu, I have a heavy responsibility. I must comb through the complex threads of destiny day after day, all for the preservation of this giant ship.

t_ch: 有些看似美好的岔路，向前望去也许是万劫不复的深渊；有些小径则深深没入了无法推算的黑暗。而到头来……
t_en: Some paths look promising, but lead to an abyss of despair. Then there are small trails that plunge deep into unimaginable darkness. And in the end...

t_ch: 我意识到，其实我们别无选择。只有踏上唯一的道路，只有奔向最优解，哪怕其中满是灰烬和苦涩。
t_en: ...I realized that we have no real choices. There is only one path to follow, only one way to the optimal solution, even if it is paved with ashes and bitterness.

t_ch: 道路，从来就只有一条。
t_en: There is only one way — and there only ever was.

t_ch: 怎么办…我感觉咱们说服不了她。
t_en: What do we do... I don't think we can persuade her...

t_ch: 换一个复杂些的沙盘推演如何？
t_en: I have a proposal — why don't we try a more advanced sandbox?

t_ch: 这个游戏你想玩多久，想翻出什么花样，本座尽可以奉陪。
t_en: However long you want to play this game, and whatever tricks you try to pull, I'll happily play along.

t_ch: …青雀小姐？
t_en: ...Miss Qingque?

t_ch: 用你们的「自由意志」把机巧带出沙盘吧。
t_en: Use your "free will" to guide the ingenium out of the sandbox.

t_ch: 这一次沙盘的路径虽有稍许变化，但一样简单得很。我想，太卜大人一定会说——
t_en: The path changed slightly, but it was just as simple. I'm sure the Master Diviner would say—

t_ch: 命运的道路只有这一条，你的选择就是早已注定的唯一答案。
t_en: Destiny only has one path. Your choices are just a predetermined solution to the puzzle.

t_ch: 我却不这么看，太卜大人，请将这沙盘重置，咱们再从头推演一回？
t_en: Actually, I don't think I agree. Master Diviner, let's reset the sandbox and give it another go, shall we?

t_ch: 再来一遍？就按着眼前的沙盘，原封不动地再来一次吗？再来一万遍结局也是注定的，因为道路从来只有一条。
t_en: Again? Do it all over again, exactly as it was? You can try it a million times over, and a million times over it is doomed to the same fate — because there is only one path.

t_ch: 我明白了…青雀，你还真是个有趣的孩子。
t_en: Hehe, I see what you're thinking, Qingque... you're an interesting one.

t_ch: 青雀小姐，我刚才偷偷盘算了一下，这道题确实只有一个解答。
t_en: Qingque, I've been thinking through the calculations for this puzzle — there really is only one solution.

t_ch: 这是犀焰设计的迷局…没用的，其中没有第二种解法。只要我们还在和它纠缠于所谓的「命运游戏」，它就是不可战胜的。
t_en: This maze was designed by Keralum. It's no use — there aren't any other solutions. As long as we're playing this so-called game of destiny, we can't win.

t_ch: 不，其中存在第二种选择。各位，永远都会有选择的。
t_en: No, there's a second choice. There will always be another choice.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你快劝劝她……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, quick, say something to persuade her...

t_ch: 我明白她的想法了…
t_en: I see where she's going with this...

t_ch: 我也不知道她有什么打算…
t_en: I have no clue what she's thinking...

t_ch: 都听你的，青雀。
t_en: It's all yours, Qingque.

t_ch: 我不是让你这么劝她……
t_en: I didn't mean like that...

t_ch: 打算开始了吗？
t_en: Ready to begin?

t_ch: 这个沙盘当然有第二种解法。{NICKNAME}，接下来，我要说出我的念头。
t_en: Of course there's another solution to this sandbox. {NICKNAME}, listen to my idea.

t_ch: 你们会帮我的，对吧？
t_en: You're all gonna help, right?

t_ch: 那还用说。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 道路不止一条…
t_en: There is more than one path...

t_ch: 说得没错。
t_en: Correct.

t_ch: 虽然不知道青雀小姐会如何解决眼前这个问题，但判官不惜一切也要完成使命。
t_en: Miss Qingque, I don't know how you're planning to solve the puzzle... but I'll support this squad to the end...

t_ch: 算我一个，青雀小姐一定是想到了什么妙招。
t_en: Count me in. I'm sure Miss Qingque has something up her sleeve.

t_ch: 嗯，只要你用得上咱们。
t_en: If we can help, I'm in.

t_ch: 需要的人手不多，两个就行。
t_en: I only need two people for my plan to work.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，能请你去沙盘上…大概那边那个位置上站一下吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you go stand in the sandbox... right about that spot over there.

t_ch: 接下来，要麻烦判官小姐在另一边那个位置上站定就好，别发抖喔。
t_en: Next, Judge Huohuo, you stand on the other side — and stop shaking!

t_ch: 成了。
t_en: All right, we're ready.

t_ch: 很好，这下第二条道路就出现了。
t_en: And now the second path appears.

t_ch: 进入沙盘
t_en: Enter Sandpit

t_ch: 离开沙盘
t_en: Exit Sandpit

t_ch: 站定
t_en: Stay put

t_ch: 被犀焰附身的太卜大人似乎比平时更容易生气，现在还是不要招惹她了。
t_en: The Keralum-possessed master diviner seems more easily enraged than she normally is. Maybe now's not the best time to bother her.

t_ch: 就像我为了输得体面一些而不停选择，太卜也在为了某个目标做出她的选择。
t_en: Even if I lose, I'll still make choices to lighten the loss — just like the Master Diviner is making choices for her own agenda.

t_ch: 这就是第二种解法，也是第二种选择，我为这个沙盘创造了不同的答案。
t_en: This is the other solution — the alternative choice. I created a new solution.

t_ch: 太卜大人，谜题不止你一人可以设计，而命运的道路上也绝不只有我一个人独行。
t_en: Master Diviner, you're not the only one who can design a puzzle, and I don't tread the path of destiny alone.

t_ch: 哼，哈哈哈哈…你以为唤人相助，就凭空创造了第二条道路？
t_en: ...Hahahaha... you think that by calling on your companions for help, you created a new path out of thin air?

t_ch: 你不过是在偷换概念。这样的路线我可以为你编织出千条万条，可它们通向的是同一个尽头，这样的选择又有什么意义？
t_en: You're just playing with semantics. I could weave a thousand routes for you, but they lead to the same destination — so what significance is there in having a choice?

t_ch: 你根本不明白青雀的意思。
t_en: You don't even understand what Qingque is saying.

t_ch: 妖物，你呀你，完全不懂人类。
t_en: Creatures like you don't understand humans.

t_ch: 宇宙嘛，就像牌桌上的琼玉牌一样。洗牌完毕时，胜负大势往往早已注定。
t_en: The universe is like jade tiles on a game table. When the tiles are shuffled, the outcome is already predetermined.

t_ch: 虽然桌上打出了什么牌，牌山上还剩什么牌，旁边的人想做什么牌型，这些统统都是能算的。可是如果只有计算，一切就没那味儿了。
t_en: Which tiles can be played, which tiles are leftover, what kind of hand the person next to you is trying to make — all of these can be determined through calculations. But if everything is just a calculation, the game loses its flavor.

t_ch: 随机应变，兵来将挡，曲曲折折，山穷水复。是一些些计算，一些些未知，一些些失控构成了琼玉牌，也就是生活的真趣。
t_en: Randomness and adaptability, ambushing your opponent, twists and turns, the balance of the elements — it's a mix of calculations, unknowns, and loss of complete control that makes Celestial Jade the kind of game it is — and those are the same things that give life its color and vibrance.
